zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall於1469年出生在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾時，她的父親John Jacob Abarough III24歲，母親Elizabeth Danvers20歲。她和John Alexander Webb III至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1533年在英國瓦維其可爾史達科特 奧 阿沃享年64歲。

nameMeanings.name: Arden
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name, either from the district of Arden in Warwickshire or from Arden in Bredbury (Cheshire). The first placename is thought to be derived from a Celtic word meaning ‘high place’, cognate with Ardennes, the name of a forested region on the borders between northeastern France and eastern Belgium. The second derives from Old English hēah ‘high’ + worthign ‘enclosed settlement’.

variants.name: Harden
meanings: English: variant of Arden with prosthetic H-.

English: habitational name, either from Haredene Wood in East Tisbury (Wiltshire), or from Haredon (in Ugborough, Devon). The first placename probably derives from Old English hara ‘hare’ + denu ‘valley’. The second is from Old English hara + dūn ‘hare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall was born in 1469, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Jacob Abarough III, was 24 and her mother, Elizabeth Danvers, was 20. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with John Alexander Webb III. She died in 1533, in Stratford on Avon, Warwickshire, England, at the age of 64.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Arden Dame of Parkhall(이)가 1469년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Jacob Abarough III(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Danvers(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 John Alexander Webb III(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1533년 영국 워윅실르 스트라트퍼드  아폰에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.
